VCCAUX.parameter: Auxiliary Supply Voltage Bank 0, Bank 1, Bank 2, Bank 6, Bank 7

VCCAUXH3/4/.parameter: Auxiliary Supply Voltage Bank 3, Bank 4, Bank 5

VCCAUXA.parameter: Auxiliary Supply Voltage for core logic

VCCIO.parameter: I/O Driver Supply Voltage VCCIO = 3.3 V, Bank 0, Bank 1, Bank 2, Bank 6, Bank 7

SerDes Block External Power Supplies.parameter: VCCSD0 Supply Voltage for SerDes Block and SerDes I/O

lattice_VIH_LVCMOS25.raw: 3.3 V to meet the VIH and VIL requirements, but there is additional current drawn on VCCIO. Hysteresis has to be disabled

lattice_VIL_LVCMOS25.raw: 3.3 V to meet the VIH and VIL requirements, but there is additional current drawn on VCCIO. Hysteresis has to be disabled

lattice_VCCO_SLVS.raw: in SLVS. D-PHY with HS and LP modes supported needs to be placed in banks with VCCIO voltage = 1.2 V. Soft MIPI D-PHY LP input

lattice_VIH_LVCMOS10.raw: VIH (MAX) for inputs on these standards (in Bank 0, Bank 1, Bank 2, Bank 6, and Bank 7) can go up to 3.465 V if the input clamp is

lattice_VCCO_LVDS.raw: input voltage cannot exceed the bank VCCIO voltage. LVDS output buffer is powered by the Bank VCCIO at 1.8 V.

power_integrity.desc: All power and ground pins must be connected

config_pins.desc: Mandatory config pins (PROGRAMN, INITN, DONE, CCLK) must be connected

vcco_bank_consistency.desc: All IO in same bank must use compatible IO standards sharing same VCCIO

diff_pair_integrity.desc: Differential pairs must be used together or not at all

unused_io.desc: Unused IO pins should not be left floating

jtag_pins.desc: JTAG pins (TCK/TMS/TDI/TDO) should be properly connected or have pull resistors

high_speed_serial.review_note: CrossLink-NX exports only the single-channel SerDes topology here; protocol selection must still be matched against the chosen Lattice IP/reference design.

SGMII.note: 1G serial Ethernet PHY-side links commonly use a 125 MHz differential reference clock.

1000BASE-X.note: 1G serial Ethernet PHY-side links commonly use a 125 MHz differential reference clock.

PCIe Gen1.note: PCI Express reference clocks are typically implemented as 100 MHz differential HCSL or translated equivalents.

PCIe Gen2.note: PCI Express reference clocks are typically implemented as 100 MHz differential HCSL or translated equivalents.

refclk_requirements.selection_note: Refclk frequency, clock standard, jitter budget, and pair-to-quad mapping must be frozen against the selected protocol before schematic sign-off.

lane_group_mappings.selection_note: Freeze the protocol/IP assignment for this lane group and bind it to one of the mapped refclk pairs before schematic sign-off.

refclk_requirements.review_note: CrossLink-NX exports only the single-channel SerDes topology here; protocol selection must still be matched against the chosen Lattice IP/reference design.

abs_VCCA_DPHY0, 1.parameter: Analog Supply Voltage for Hardened D-PHY

abs_VCC_DPHY0, 1.parameter: Digital Supply Voltage for Hardened D-PHY

abs_Input or I/O Voltage Applied, Bank 0.symbol: Input or I/O Voltage Applied, Bank 0, Bank 1,Bank 2, Bank 6, Bank 7
